Answer on prg06ic-008 (cutover-flip mid-grace-period hazard) — gated, lane not blocked. This cross-cutting hazard becomes a HARD PRECONDITION on WP-FLAG (WI-779): the flip must not happen until every in-flight grace-period / multi-day / scheduled flow that can span the flip window is safe across a mode change. (1) Run the sibling audit BEFORE WP-FLAG — at minimum scheduled exports (WP-4/WI-773), grace-period + trial billing transitions (WP-7), and any Inngest flow that persists a pending decision across the flip window (WP-8); classify each and prove it with a regression test, using the WP-4 deletion fix as the template (pin identity mode at schedule, use it for every step). (2) Prefer MODE-PINNING as the PRIMARY mitigation, not drain/quiesce. Rationale: the hazard is symmetric (flip OR rollback misroutes), and an unplanned/emergency rollback cannot be drained-for in advance, so only schedule-time mode-pinning protects BOTH the planned flip and an unplanned rollback. Drain/quiesce is a complement — sufficient only for a flow that genuinely cannot be pinned AND where rollback is ruled out. (3) Bake this precondition into WP-FLAG (WI-779) Acceptance Criteria, and capture the drain/pin procedure + the audited flow list in the cutover runbook (author the runbook if it does not yet exist — treat it as a WP-FLAG / WI-586 close-artifact).
